title: JSON Schema for Atmos Stack Manifest files. Version 1.0. https://atmos.tools

name.description: Logical name for this stack. Takes precedence over name_template and name_pattern from atmos.yaml.

secrets.description: Stack-level (global) secret declarations and providers. Declared here, a secret is stack-scoped: stored once and shared by every component instance in the stack, unless a component re-declares it (which pulls it to instance scope).

ttl.description: Cache duration for the cloned source repo of a remote (git) import. When set, the clone is reused across Atmos invocations until it expires (e.g. with a warm CI cache). Within a single invocation a source repo is cloned at most once regardless of TTL. Examples: '0s', '5m', '1h', '7d', 'daily'.

kubernetes_paths.title: kubernetes_paths
kubernetes_paths.description: Manifest files or directories relative to the Kubernetes component directory

kubernetes_manifests.title: kubernetes_manifests
kubernetes_manifests.description: Inline Kubernetes manifests rendered and deployed with the component

kubernetes_manifest.title: kubernetes_manifest
kubernetes_manifest.description: Kubernetes API object. Atmos validates only the structural fields it requires before passing the object to the Kubernetes API.

kubernetes_render.title: kubernetes_render
kubernetes_render.description: Render output configuration for Kubernetes manifests

provision.title: provision
provision.description: Provisioning configuration such as workdir or delivery targets

workdir.description: Working directory provisioning settings for this component

default.description: Name of the provision target used by apply/deploy when --target is not given

targets.description: Named delivery destinations for rendered artifacts, keyed by target name

repository.description: For kind 'git': name of a top-level git.repositories.<name> to publish to

path.description: For kind 'git': destination path inside the deployment repository (supports Go templates)

identity.description: Atmos Auth identity used for the delivery

enabled.description: Publish via pull request (not yet supported by the cli git provider)

dependencies.title: dependencies
dependencies.description: Component, file, or folder dependencies used by affected detection and DAG execution

name.description: Logical component identity used for workspace key prefix auto-generation. Inheritable from base components.

enabled.description: Flag to enable or disable the component

oneOf.description: A list of Atmos components that the current component inherits from
Custom configuration per component. Inherited when stacks.inherit.metadata is enabled (default).
oneOf.title: custom

locked.description: Flag to lock the component and prevent modifications while allowing read operations

module_paths.description: List of paths to validation modules

retry.title: retry
retry.description: Per-component retry configuration. When `conditions` patterns match captured subprocess output, the failing command is retried with the configured backoff. Without `conditions`, no retry is attempted.

max_attempts.description: Maximum number of attempts (including the first). Omit for unlimited; defaults to 1 when retry is unset entirely.

backoff_strategy.description: How delays grow between attempts. Defaults to exponential when initial_delay is set.

initial_delay.description: Delay before the second attempt as a Go duration string, e.g. "2s", "500ms".

max_delay.description: Upper bound on any single delay (caps exponential growth), e.g. "30s".

max_elapsed_time.description: Total time budget across all attempts, e.g. "5m". After this the most recent error is returned.

multiplier.description: Growth multiplier for exponential backoff. Defaults to 2.0.

random_jitter.description: Fractional jitter applied to each delay (0–1).

conditions.description: Regex patterns matched against captured stdout/stderr. Only errors whose output matches at least one pattern are retried. Patterns may be wrapped in `/.../` for readability.

terraform_rc.title: terraform_rc
terraform_rc.description: Terraform/OpenTofu CLI configuration (.terraformrc / .tofurc) rendered by Atmos and exposed to the subprocess via TF_CLI_CONFIG_FILE and TOFU_CLI_CONFIG_FILE. A near-opaque passthrough: keys map directly to CLI-config directives (provider_installation, host, credentials, plugin_cache_dir, ...), so new directives need no Atmos schema change.

enabled.description: Enable rendering of the Terraform CLI configuration. Defaults to false.

terraform_cache.title: terraform_cache
terraform_cache.description: Terraform registry cache configuration. Transparently caches providers and modules through an ephemeral local network-mirror proxy. Execution-environment (runner) configuration, not stack data.

enabled.description: Enable the registry cache. Defaults to false.

location.description: Cache root. Defaults to the XDG cache directory under terraform/registry (e.g. ~/.cache/atmos/terraform/registry).

metadata_ttl.description: Time-to-live for registry metadata as a Go duration string. Defaults to 24h.

stale_while_revalidate.description: Window during which stale metadata may be served while revalidating, as a Go duration string. Defaults to 168h.

mirror.description: Eager multi-platform pre-seeding via `atmos terraform cache mirror` (wraps `terraform providers mirror`).

enabled.description: Express fleet pre-seeding policy. The `cache mirror` command runs regardless. Defaults to false.

terraform_platforms.title: terraform_platforms
terraform_platforms.description: Target platforms a project builds for, as <os>_<arch> (e.g. linux_amd64, darwin_arm64). Consumed by `atmos terraform cache mirror` for eager pre-seeding and by the automatic post-init `providers lock` that keeps .terraform.lock.hcl complete across platforms. Empty defaults to the host platform.

generate.title: generate
generate.description: Generate section for declarative file generation. Keys are filenames, values are content (string for literal template, object for serialized output based on file extension).

oneOf.description: Literal template content written as-is (supports Go templates)
Object content serialized based on file extension (.json, .yaml, .hcl, .tf)

hooks.title: hooks
hooks.description: Hooks section. Each key names a hook; the value sets its kind, the lifecycle events it fires on, and kind-specific settings.

kind.description: Selects the engine that runs this hook.

command.description: Binary to execute (command kind); also the legacy alias for kind (e.g. 'command: store').

events.description: Lifecycle events this hook fires on (e.g. after.terraform.apply). The legacy hyphenated form (e.g. after-terraform-apply) is also accepted. Empty matches all events.

on_failure.description: Failure mode when the hook errors.

when.description: Run this hook based on the operation outcome: success (default), failure, or always.

type.description: Step type to run (step kind): any registered workflow/custom-command step type, e.g. container, http, toast, log.

with.description: Parameters for the step named by 'type' (step kind). Validated by the step handler at run time.

retry.description: Retry policy wrapping step execution (step kind); same schema as a workflow step's retry block.

required_providers.title: required_providers
required_providers.description: Terraform required providers section for version pinning
